Mask of Superiority  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FACE
AC: 15
STR: +10 DEX: +10 STA: +9 CHA: +3 WIS: +4 INT: +4 AGI: +4 HP: +45
SV FIRE: +2 SV DISEASE: +2 SV COLD: +2 SV MAGIC: +2 SV POISON: +2
WT: 10.0 Size: TINY
Class: WAR
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Mask of Superiority
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FACE
AC: 15
STR: +10 DEX: +10 STA: +9 CHA: +3 WIS: +4 INT: +4 AGI: +4 HP: +45
SV FIRE: +2 SV DISEASE: +2 SV COLD: +2 SV MAGIC: +2 SV POISON: +2
WT: 10.0 Size: TINY
Class: WAR
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-16 11:58:05
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

4 posts
I have this mask, it owns...9 sta and 45hp and 15ac just own. no question about it. Wow...10 weight, it doesnt really effect a warrior. :)

# Aug 02 2001 at 8:35 AM Rating: Decent

91 posts
very very nice
▲ Page top
Str vs. Encumberment
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 6:36 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757823993
9 posts
being encumbered has nothing to do with your attack rating, it only affects your ac/agility. Str raises I think 1 attack point every like 2.5 str points or something i think it was.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 02 2001 at 7:19 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) In fact your encumberance rating does lower your atk and causes you to miss more. Furthermore being fatgued also does this which is a depleation of your stamina. When your stamina becomes depleated you become encumbered also. I don't know why you haven't realized this, maybe you should parce yourself while encumbered or fatigued. Do this: turn on atk; jump till your stamina is out; then duel someone and parce yourself. Now duel at full stamina. Now do the same thing with half the str or just below encumberance.
RE: Str vs. Encumberment
QuoteReply
# Mar 28 2002 at 9:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361598394
16 posts
You have no penalty if your stamina is above 100. No agi/dex drop, no ac drop, no attack drop.
Now, if your yellow bar runs out AND your stamina is below 100, yes you do have major stats penalty.

Anyway, by the time you can hope killing this kind of mob, your stamina should be close to 200.. (for a warrior that is). My sta is 75 naked (fear being a dark elf warrior) and 159 gear on and unbuffed.

If you are overweight, you loose agi and since agi modify the avoidance part of AC, you loose AC. But afaik you don't loose anything in fatigue bar.

▲ Page top
RE: Str vs. Encumberment
QuoteReply
# Aug 06 2001 at 1:30 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247845609
Scholar
73 posts
Why would you bother logging combat text, and running a log file parser? You can just open your inventory and look at your stats. If your fatigue bar has run out (fighting underwater, weilding heavy weapons, etc) and your stamina statistic is low, you will lose agi and dex. If you are encumbered, you will lose agi. And just fyi, adding more wt doesn't necessarily make you encumbered, unless it puts you over your max carrying weight. The more wt you have, the more damage you take from falling, but unless you go over your max weight you won't notice the difference unless you try jumping off a cliff. =P
▲ Page top
RE: Str vs. Encumberment
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 9:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361593319
34 posts
1 str = about 1 attack... caus i know a TBB which has 15 str gives 14 attack. at lower lvls it does have the same ratio that is what it is for me and i'm a 47 warrior
▲ Page top
Weight
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 2:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750106032
View User Forum
Scholar
221 posts
Understanding that the STR bonus this gives would offset it's weight encumbrance-wise, I still cannot help but notice that 10.0 is a bit extreme. I don't play a tank so maybe someone can enlighten me as to how the STR encumbrance versus STR-based attack bonus plays out?

Thayn
▲ Page top
RE: Weight
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2003 at 12:08 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256062452
18 posts
61 War here, got this mask tonight. Weight is a push with the str, and I am usually running unbuffed about 124/186, now that is 130/196, plenty of room :)

Edited, Wed Jan 15 23:42:48 2003
▲ Page top
re: Humor Me
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 2:12 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762984907
13 posts
well, int plays an important role for trade skills and how fast you learn your skills.
Gnome warriors would have already maxed out their skills while Ogres or Trolls are still trying to grasp the idea of the skill. You have became better at 1 handed slashing
(50) while the gnome warrior has (101) and for wisdom, I'll have to agree with you on that one. Why do tanks need wisdom for?
▲ Page top
RE: re: Humor Me
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 5:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248459345
31 posts
Intelligence effects how fast you learn a trade skill while wisdom effects how often you suceed or fail at making an item through the skill. That is stated in the everquest faq book which i own.
▲ Page top
RE: re: Humor Me
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 1:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591752020117
6 posts
That FAQ book is wrong in a number of places, and this is one of them.

According to Verant's Tradeskill guy (the one most recently responsible for coding tradeskill changes) the only factors on the chance of success in a Tradeskill are the difficulty of the item and your level in the necessary skill.

Stats are used purely for determining the chance of increasing the skill. Primarily, they check the higher of intelligence or wisdom. For some skills, there is an alternate secondary stat that adds to the chance of increaseing the skill.

They discuss this extensively on www.eqtraders.com
▲ Page top
RE: re: Humor Me
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 5:11 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750388498
100 posts
Well if the warrior's wis is higher than his int (which it will probably be for everyone but gnomes and dark elves) then that stats is what determines how easily you skill up. Verant might as well have made one stat called mind but that wouldn't have looked good to the D&D crazed masses.
Of which I am one (centaur bard - kickass!)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 01 2001 at 1:22 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) What were they thinking putting Wis and Int on a warrior item. We dont' need smart tanks. If they get thier Int up higher they soon are going to realize that casters are just using them to stay alive, and that thier death toll has been really adding up. AHHHh what is EQ coming to.
RE: Humor me
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 8:14 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751231908
90 posts
By the time they can get these, why would you need trade skills? What are you going to make for yourself better than your other armor. And if you want it for money making, you are quite mistaken considering that a warrior with that could venture down to lguk or solb, get a druid or cleric friend and own the lord or king room for some quick cash (I would atleast). Also, if they are making something for their newbies, I'm sure they could buy it instead of spending time on the tradeskills. And everyone out their saying that there is alot of pride being master, there isnt.
▲ Page top
RE: Humor me
QuoteReply
# Aug 08 2001 at 10:08 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248395208
Scholar
32 posts
"And everyone out their saying that there is alot of pride being master, there isnt."

Just my opinion, but I disagree with this statement. I take great pride in being a master at the tradeskills I practice. Not everyone has the patience, dedication or drive to spend the time and PP it takes to earn this title.

It is true that I can make more money in 4 hours hunting hill giants in Rathe Mountains than I can by smithing or tailoring or fletching, but my options are more diverse because of the time and effort expended in tradeskills.

Depsite what some believe, I am of the opinion that tradeskills are worthwhile endeavors, and being a "master" is worthy of a degree of respect.
▲ Page top
RE: Humor me
QuoteReply

__DEL__1593361362547
103 posts
actually with int warriors learn more skills faster than normally useful if your a dorf of troll..............

Aye, a nice item indeed...the saves are kinda low, I could see someone swapping it out on raids for better resist gear(though I would guess that a warrior would have their str maxed already by the time they could kill Lendiniara), still nice to have for the 15ac and 45hps...its gotta be one of the higher ac masks for warriors. Not everything can be uber, but this is still pretty nice and something I wouldn't mind having for my warrior! LOL!!

the 10.o stones dont mean much my 65 warrior has 256 str unbuffed and I carry 113 stones

if your a warrior that cable of getting this and complane about the 10.0 stone go get you some whate reduckion bags for those spare wepions in your invetory

but more on seress not if you cant aford 10.0 for a face pice this nice you need to bank your smithing bags befor going on raid
